summary: The term 'hapten' refers to small molecules without intrinsic immunoactivity; these gain this capability only when bound or linked to proteins. Thus, haptens need to combine with other antigens such as proteins before triggering immunity responses effectively. \boxed{\text{Haptens are non-protein entities requiring linkage with large biomolecules to induce an immune reaction}} Summary: A hapten is a small, non-immunogenic compound that requires attachment to a carrier protein to elicit an immune response due to its inability to provoke one by itself. Hence, it needs combination with proteins to achieve full immunogenic potential.\boxed{\text{A hapten necessitates binding to a protein to stimulate an immune system response since it cannot do so independently. }}
